name: Design Collaterals
status: documented — catalogue only, not a rules section
description: Applied proof-of-system: real production examples showing Logo, Colour, Typography, Imagery, Icons, and Graphic Design working together across formats. Unlike the other sections, this one doesn't introduce new rules — it demonstrates the existing rules in production, and includes at least one campaign redesign (a visible before/after iteration).
keyRule: Use this section as a reference gallery when evaluating whether a new piece of collateral matches the system — not as a source of new rules. If something here doesn't match a rule documented elsewhere in brand/, the rule elsewhere wins; flag the inconsistency rather than treating the example as license to deviate.
formatsShown: Digital ads — Redesign 1 (a visible campaign iteration, shown alongside the original)
doThis: Check a new deliverable against the closest matching format shown here before finalizing.
Treat the 'Redesign 1' ads as the current direction, not the earlier version shown next to them.
dontDoThis: Don't treat an applied example as an exception to a documented rule — raise it instead.
Don't copy layout choices from these examples as if they were prescriptive templates; they're proof of the system, not a template library.
relationToProductTokens: None directly — this is a marketing-collateral gallery, not a rules or token source.
